title: fritillaria delphinensis subsp. moggridgei
intro: Fritillaria delphinensis subsp. moggridgei, also known as the Mongolian fritillary, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family. It is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including the presence of the alkaloids peimine and peiminine, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and respiratory issues, and promoting the recovery of patients with pneumonia and bronchitis. Fritillaria delphinensis subsp. moggridgei can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and powders, which are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
